channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Saka trains alone as England prepare for Ghana game
item.description: Bukayo Saka was the only player not present in group training as England continued their preparations for their second World Cup match against Ghana.

item.title: Why Kane is different at this World Cup - Shearer
item.description: Former England captain Alan Shearer explains what has changed for Harry Kane since his struggles at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.

item.title: Referee told McGinn penalty denied as ball going out of play
item.description: Referee Ilgiz Tantashev told John McGinn he turned down his claim for a penalty against Morocco because the ball was going out of play, the Scotland midfielder's brother, Stephen, says.

item.title: Curacao's trailblazer doctor on football's big stage
item.description: Dr Suzanne Huurman is head of medical staff for Curacao's national men's side - the World Cup's smallest ever team by population and size.

item.title: How does qualification for the World Cup knockout stage work?
item.description: With the second round of group-stage matches under way, we assess who can play who in the knockout rounds - and how they can get there.

item.title: Gakpo fires home the 100th goal of this World Cup
item.description: Cody Gakpo scores the 100th goal of this World Cup as he his second of the game to put Netherlands 4-0 ahead against Sweden.

item.title: Who should Scotland fans be cheering on in next few days?
item.description: Uzbeks to beat DR Congo? Argentina to run riot? Favours from England? BBC Scotland weighs up the permutations needed for Scotland to progress.

item.title: Boston bounce sends Scotland to Miami with hope and no fear
item.description: Scotland and their fans head to Miami for a World Cup date with destiny after making memories in Boston to last a lifetime.

item.title: Oh, brother! The sibling rivals lining up for different World Cup teams
item.description: Sibling rivals are lining up for different teams at the World Cup, with seven pairs of brothers in total at this tournament.

item.title: 'Everything I do is for you' - how tragedy is driving Diomande
item.description: BBC Sport explores how exciting young Ivory Coast Yan Diomande is determined to succeed at the World Cup in honour of his sister.

item.title: 'Star in the making' - What is Man City's plan for forgotten man Reis?
item.description: Manchester City are still awaiting the arrival of their new manager but forgotten defender Vitor Reis will be back in the building following his season-long loan spell in La Liga.

item.title: Brobbey scores twice against Sweden
item.description: Brian Brobbey scores twice in the first half in Netherland's World Cup match with Sweden.

item.title: World Cup first as Almiron sent off for covering mouth
item.description: Watch Miguel Almiron become the first player to be sent off under new rules preventing players from covering their mouths during a game at the 2026 World Cup, with the Paraguay midfielder shown red in a win over Turkey.

item.title: Scotland fall to narrow defeat against Morocco
item.description: Scotland concede after 71 seconds and fall to a World Cup defeat against a classy Morocco, but their hopes of a historic knock-out place remain in their own hands with one group game to go.

item.title: Cunha scores twice as Brazil beat Haiti
item.description: Manchester United forward Matheus Cunha scores twice as Brazil comfortably beat Haiti - who become the first team to be eliminated from the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: 10-man Paraguay knock out Turkey despite Almiron red card
item.description: Watch highlights as 10-man Paraguay win despite Miguel Almiron's red card, eliminating Turkey from the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: World Cup still waits for real Brazil to show up
item.description: While rivals Argentina and France have impressed in the United States, Brazil have struggled to hit top form - despite a 3-0 win over minnows Haiti.

item.title: Almiron first player sent off for covering mouth
item.description: Paraguay's Miguel Almiron is the first player to be shown a red card for covering his mouth while speaking to an opponent - doing so during the World Cup match against Turkey.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 13
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Flawed but relentless Scotland show themselves as men of substance
item.description: Morocco threatened to blow Scotland away early on, but Steve Clarke's squad showed their resilience despite defeat, writes Tom English.

item.title: White House in talks over Iran travel restrictions
item.description: The White House is considering changing the strict travel restrictions Iran are facing at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: What next for McAtee amid Anderson uncertainty?
item.description: After struggling to break through at Nottingham Forest last season, will James McAtee get his chance if Elliot Anderson leaves for Manchester City?

item.title: How to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: The Super Bowl winner's son stepping into spotlight with USA
item.description: Alex Freeman is stepping out of the shadows of his Super Bowl-winning father to forge his own path in football.

item.title: Why are so many World Cup goals being scored late?
item.description: World Cup 2026 is witnessing a surge in late goals. Longer stoppage times, tactical substitutions and hydration breaks are reshaping matches

item.title: Should Scotland have had two penalties & been playing against 10 men?
item.description: Were Scotland hard done by in Boston in losing 1-0 to Morocco in their second game of the World Cup finals?

item.title: World Cup groups separated by head-to-head records for first time
item.description: Fifa is using head-to-head records instead of group goal difference as the first tiebreaker for teams who are level on points at the World Cup.

item.title: England winger Saka unlikely to start against Ghana
item.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel hints that Bukayo Saka is unlikely to be in contention to start against Ghana on Tuesday.

item.title: Spurs to enter Wharton chase - Saturday's gossip
item.description: Tottenham are the latest club interested in Adam Wharton, Liverpool are yet to agree a deal for Yan Diomande, Fulham fail with their opening bid for Chibuike Nwaiwu, plus more.

item.title: 'Big decisions went against Scotland'
item.description: BBC Sport Scotland's Liam McLeod and James McFadden analyse the three key decisions that went against Scotland during their World Cup Group C 1-0 defeat against Morocco.

item.title: 'Nightmare start for Scotland' - Morocco score after 71 seconds
item.description: Morocco's Ismael Saibari stuns Scotland with a superb finish after just 71 seconds in their World Cup Group C game in Boston.

item.title: USA qualify for last 32 after comfortable win over Australia
item.description: The United States take a huge step towards winning their World Cup group beating Australia 2-0 with a dominant, high-energy performance in front of a boisterous sellout crowd in Seattle.

item.title: On another day that's a penalty - Clarke on McGinn claim
item.description: Scotland boss Steve Clarke believes the challenge on John McGinn by Neil El Aynaoui was the Tartan Army's strongest penalty claim against Morocco.

item.title: Canada score six but historic win marred by Kone injury
item.description: Ismael Kone's serious-looking injury left Canada manager Jesse Marsch in tears and overshadowed their first-ever World Cup finals win as they thrashed nine-man Qatar 6-0.

item.title: Did key decisions go against Scotland in Morocco match?
item.description: Should Scotland feel aggrieved after three key decisions in their World Cup Group C match against Morocco in Boston?

item.title: Morocco 'got away with one' - McGinn
item.description: Scotland's John McGinn feels Morocco "got away with one" after he was not awarded a penalty in the second half of their World Cup Group C match in Boston.

item.title: Manzambi double propels Switzerland to win against Bosnia
item.description: Substitute Johan Manzambi scores twice in a thrilling final 20 minutes, as Switzerland beat Bosnia-Herzegovina 4-1 to edge towards a place in the World Cup round of 32.

item.title: How presenter's Messi error caused outrage in Argentina
item.description: An Argentine TV presenter steps down after wrongly saying on air that Lionel Messi's father had died.

item.title: Premier League fixtures - find out your club's schedule
item.description: The Premier League fixtures are out - find out your club's schedule.

item.title: Ipswich close in on O'Neil as new head coach
item.description: Former Wolves and Bournemouth boss O'Neil close to joining Ipswich after Keiran McKenna's departure.

item.title: Stick or twist? Rashford and Guehi are Tuchel's big dilemmas
item.description: Before England's World Cup match against Ghana on Tuesday, BBC Sport's Phil McNulty looks at Thomas Tuchel's big selection dilemmas.

item.title: A heavily jeered $250m goldmine - are hydration break ads here to stay?
item.description: BBC Sport explores how hydration break ads work in other countries and how they could impact the future of football.

item.title: Nike v Adidas - the World Cup brand battle
item.description: Eye-watering budgets are nothing new, but this year both Nike and Adidas have gone bigger and bolder than ever before in the fight for World Cup attention.

item.title: Coventry play champions Arsenal in Premier League opener
item.description: Newly promoted Coventry City will travel to champions Arsenal in the first match of the 2026-27 Premier League season.

item.title: Sutton's World Cup score predictions - second group games
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the scores of the second round of group games at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: England's Barry to continue with half-time interviews
item.description: England assistant coach Anthony Barry is cleared to continue conducting televised half-time interviews during the World Cup.

item.title: Canada's Kone grateful for support after surgery
item.description: Canada midfielder Ismael Kone thanks his team-mates and fans for their support after having surgery on a broken leg.

item.title: Africa's greatest World Cup kits - pick your favourite
item.description: Your chance to rank 10 of the most colourful and iconic kits African teams have worn at the World Cup.

item.title: Fifa bans football club's submarine badge on flag
item.description: The Barrow flag is banned from World Cup stadiums as it includes a depiction of military equipment.

item.title: The celebration photo that sums up my relationship with Mbappe - Giroud
item.description: France's World Cup winner Olivier Giroud explains why iconic Les Bleus striker Kylian Mbappe is misunderstood, and seen as arrogant when he's not.

item.title: This was an England we haven't seen for years - and it was fun
item.description: Fun to watch and full of intent - this was an England we have not seen for years, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: From one to 48 - every World Cup team ranked after first game
item.description: All 48 teams at the World Cup have now played once. BBC Sport's experts have ranked them all, from best to worst. Who is top? Not defending champions Argentina.

item.title: Hydration boos and surprise results - World Cup talking points
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at the talking points from the first week of World Cup.

item.title: Project Mbappe - the road to becoming France's record scorer
item.description: Kylian Mbappe has become France's all-time record scorer, aged just 27. BBC Sport takes a closer look at how he got here.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Will three points guarantee a spot in World Cup knockouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at whether earning three points in the World Cup group stage will be enough to advance to the knockout rounds.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: How to watch highlights of World Cup matches
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how you can watch match highlights and full match replays during the 2026 Fifa World Cup

item.title: Why are footballers wearing pink boots at the World Cup?
item.description: The opening match of the 2026 World Cup was dominated by players wearing pink boots - but why is the colour so prevalent in football at the moment?

item.title: What are the deadlines for World Cup squads?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the deadlines by which teams must name their final squads for the 2026 World Cup

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Crisis club or chance to reinvent - where does McInnes exit leave Hearts?
item.description: Derek McInnes has left Heart of Midlothian for Rangers, so how will the Scottish Premiership runners-up react to losing their head coach?

item.title: Calvin Harris & Commonwealths force Monday start to Celtic's title defence
item.description: Celtic open the 2026-27 Scottish Premiership season at home to Dundee on 3 August, with the champions saying they are disappointed to be starting on a Monday evening.

item.title: Motherwell appoint Johansson as Askou successor - who is he?
item.description: Alfred Johansson is the new manager at Motherwell, with the 35-year-old Swede aiming to build on the success of last season at Fir Park.

item.title: Nearly man McInnes finally has Rangers job he really always wanted
item.description: Why has Derek McInnes swapped nearly winning the Scottish title with Heart of Midlothian for a Rangers side who finished behind them in third last season?

item.title: Earps 'doesn't know' if she's ready for spotlight again
item.description: Former England goalkeeper Mary Earps does not know if she is ready to be back in the spotlight after signing for the London City Lionesses.

item.title: London City Lionesses sign ex-England keeper Earps
item.description: Former England goalkeeper Mary Earps joins London City Lionesses on a two-year deal after leaving Paris St-Germain.

item.title: Beever-Jones set to sign new deal at Chelsea
item.description: England international Aggie Beever-Jones is expected to stay at Chelsea this summer with a new deal extending her stay.

item.title: Lionesses will face Greece in World Cup play-offs
item.description: England will face Greece in round one of the 2027 Women's World Cup play-offs, with Northern Ireland, Scotland and Wales also learning their opponents.

item.title: 'I want courage not fear' - Wales boss on World Cup play-offs
item.description: Wales must beat Albania and either Romania or Norway in successive two-legged play-off ties to reach next summer's Women's World Cup.

item.title: Tottenham sign Scotland forward Hanson from Villa
item.description: Tottenham have signed Scotland international Kirsty Hanson from Aston Villa for an undisclosed fee.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
